prompt: Infrastructure teams deploy Windows and Linux VMs in AWS and Azure alongside on-premises servers. How should HAM track those hybrid assets?
choices: Combine Discovery and cloud inventory feeds with normalized models and lifecycle states for virtual and physical hardware assets
Track only on-premises serial numbers and ignore cloud instances in the asset table
Convert every VM into a consumable quantity without CI or asset linkage
Disable model normalization for cloud-discovered records to speed imports
explanation: Modern HAM programs reconcile Discovery and cloud source data into governed hardware models with consistent lifecycle tracking.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: A SaaS team procures 5,000 collaboration seats while HAM manages corporate laptops. Which ITAM approach prevents duplicate spend and orphan assignments?
choices: Coordinate SAM subscription records with HAM hardware assets through shared requester, cost center, and lifecycle governance
Track SaaS seats only in spreadsheets outside ServiceNow to avoid SAM licensing
Disable HAM catalog fulfillment whenever SAM records exist for the same user
Retire laptop assets automatically when any SaaS subscription is activated
explanation: Integrated ITAM connects SAM subscription management with HAM hardware custody and financial ownership for complete spend visibility.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: Audit findings show laptops assigned without a documented owner or custodian. Which governance framework should HAM implement?
choices: Define asset owner, custodian, and requestor roles with workflow enforcement and exception approval for policy deviations
Allow any user with the itil role to edit asset ownership fields without approval
Remove ownership fields from asset forms to simplify data entry
Assign every asset to a single generic user account permanently
explanation: Asset governance assigns accountable roles and controlled workflows so custody and financial responsibility remain auditable.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: Security policy requires certified destruction of storage media when high-risk laptops retire. How should HAM support compliance?
choices: Use governed retirement workflows that capture disposal vendor certificates and link evidence to the asset record
Delete asset records immediately without disposal documentation to reduce table size
Convert retired laptops back to In stock without sanitization checks
Track disposal only in email threads without updating lifecycle states
explanation: Disposal governance depends on verifiable retirement records showing policy steps and vendor certification were followed.
domain: Operational Integration of IT Asset Management Processes

prompt: Finance capitalizes 10,000 laptops using five-year straight-line depreciation. Which HAM configuration supports accurate monthly depreciation postings?
choices: Depreciation methods and schedules on hardware models or assets aligned to the capitalization policy and useful life
Manual journal entries only with no depreciation fields on asset records
Immediate full expense at receipt with no lifecycle financial tracking
Depreciation calculated from discovery probe timestamps rather than purchase dates
explanation: HAM depreciation configuration on models and assets supports consistent financial treatment across large standardized hardware estates.
